title: Document agent-trust boundary in docs
goal: Add a short Security section (README + docs) documenting the SEC-1 trust boundary: a git-shared/cloned .mini/ is untrusted input because its content is fed into the agent's prompts, so avoid running 'mini auto' unattended (acceptEdits) on a repo you didn't author or haven't reviewed, with the human 'done' checkpoint named as the main safety net.

steps.title: New docs/security.md trust-boundary guide
steps.detail: Standalone page: threat model (mini feeds git-shared .mini/ content — project.md, phase goals, discuss/run reports — into the agent's prompts), the SEC-1 risk (cloned/pulled repo with poisoned .mini/ is a prompt-injection vector when running 'mini auto' in acceptEdits), concrete mitigations (don't run 'mini auto' unattended on un-reviewed repos; treat untrusted .mini/ as untrusted input), and that the human 'done' checkpoint is the main safety net. Cites .mini/security/range-1-25.md (SEC-1).

steps.title: Short Security note in README
steps.detail: One short paragraph under a new ## Security heading (placed near 'What gets sent to Claude'), stating the trust boundary in 2–3 sentences and linking to docs/security.md. Keeps README slim per phases 131–146.

steps.title: Cross-link from docs index, FAQ and auto
steps.detail: Add docs/security.md to the 'Concepts & guides' list in docs/README.md; add a one-line pointer where acceptEdits/unattended runs are already discussed (docs/faq.md and/or docs/non-interactive/auto.md) so the warning is reachable from the auto docs.

steps.title: Accuracy + link verification
steps.detail: Verify every claim against the code paths in the SEC-1 finding (acceptEdits flow, prompt assembly from .mini/), confirm all new relative links resolve, and check no inline styles/scripts were introduced.

resolvedVerify: Read the README Security paragraph and docs/security.md for tone/clarity

autoCommit.subject: Phase 173: Document agent-trust boundary in docs
